Bash(git commit -m "$(cat <<''COMMITMSG''
Implement Phase 1 Task 2: Complete folder structure and configuration

This commit implements Task 2 from Phase 1 Development Plan, establishing
the complete frontend architecture and configuration for the React application.

## What''s Included:

### Folder Structure
- Complete src/ directory with organized architecture
- 8 service layer files (auth, user, avatar, dress, pose, tryOn, appointment, apiClient)
- 7 TypeScript type definition files + barrel export
- 4 custom React hooks (useAuth, useApi, useImageUpload, useDebounce)
- 2 context providers (AuthContext, AppContext)
- 20+ component placeholders across layout, shared, auth, avatar, tryon, gallery, appointments
- 6 page components (Auth, AvatarCreation, TryOn, Gallery, Appointments, Share)
- 4 utility files (validators, formatters, errorHandlers, imageUtils)
- 3 constant files (routes, storageKeys, errorMessages in Spanish)

### Configuration Files
- .env.development with all API endpoints for local development
- .env.production with production API URLs
- .env.example as template for developers
- src/config/envConfig.ts with environment variable validation
- src/config/apiConfig.ts for API client configuration
- src/config/appConfig.ts for application-wide constants
- src/vite-env.d.ts for TypeScript environment types

### Main Application Files
- src/App.tsx - Root component
- src/main.tsx - Application entry point
- src/router.tsx - Router placeholder
- src/styles/globals.css - Global styles with Tailwind

### Documentation Updates
- Updated CLAUDE.md with enhanced Git/GitFlow guidelines
- Updated Phase 1 document marking Task 2 as completed (2/6 tasks done)
- Created Phase 1.1 - Next Steps.txt with continuation plan

## Technical Highlights:
- All files include JSDoc comments explaining their purpose
- Proper TypeScript types throughout (strict mode ready)
- ES Module imports with .js extensions
- Named exports convention (except App.tsx for compatibility)
- Spanish error messages for user-facing content
- Mobile-first responsive design approach
- All API endpoints configurable via environment variables

## Next Steps:
Phase 1.1 ready to implement:
- Task 3: API Client with interceptors
- Task 4: Auth Flow (Phone + SMS)
- Task 5: Basic Routing with protected routes
- Task 6: Layout Components (Header, Navigation)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
COMMITMSG
)")

Bash(git commit -m "$(cat <<''EOF''
Implement Phase 1.1 Part 1: API Client + Shared Components

This commit implements the first part of Phase 1.1 focusing on foundation
infrastructure and reusable UI components validated against the Backend API
Specification Phase 1.

## Architecture Decision

**DressId External Parameter**: Documented critical architectural decision that
dressId will ALWAYS be provided via URL parameter from external catalog. NO
dress selector will ever be implemented. See Architecture Decision document
for full rationale and implementation details.

## Backend API Alignment

- Updated all services to match Backend API Specification Phase 1
- Removed endpoints not available in Phase 1 (dresses, poses, share, book)
- Updated endpoint paths (e.g., /user/upload instead of /user/upload-photo)
- Services now throw clear errors for Phase 2-only functionality
- Phase 1 plan updated to reflect validated Backend API endpoints only

## API Client Enhancements (Task 3 Complete)

- ✅ Retry logic with exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s) for network/5xx errors
- ✅ Smart error detection (retries network/5xx, skips 4xx client errors)
- ✅ Enhanced logging with timestamps for development debugging
- ✅ Improved 401 handling (clears all auth data + redirects)
- ✅ Proper 403/404 handling with warnings
- ✅ TypeScript strict types with RetryConfig interface

## Shared Components (Task 6 Part 1 Complete)

### Button Component
- 3 variants: primary (brand #8C6F5A), secondary, danger
- 3 sizes: sm, md, lg
- Loading state with animated spinner
- Disabled state with opacity
- Full width option
- Fully typed with ButtonProps interface

### Input Component
- 4 input types: text, email, tel, number
- Label support with proper accessibility (htmlFor + id)
- Error state with red border + error message display
- Icon support (left/right positioned)
- Disabled state
- Auto-focus option
- Max length validation
- Fully accessible (aria-invalid, aria-describedby)

### Loader Component
- Animated spinner with brand color (#8C6F5A)
- 3 sizes: sm, md, lg
- Inline or centered display modes
- Optional loading text
- Reusable for buttons, pages, and overlays

## Service Layer Updates

All services updated for Phase 1 consistency:
- authService: Verified endpoints match API spec
- userService: Updated upload endpoint, disabled updateProfile for Phase 1
- avatarService: Removed uploadAvatar, added generateAvatar with prompt
- tryOnService: Removed getTryOnById (not in Phase 1)
- appointmentService: Removed bookAppointment (Phase 2)
- dressService: Marked as Phase 2-only with clear error messages
- poseService: Marked as Phase 2-only with clear error messages

## Configuration Updates

- envConfig.ts: Removed Phase 2 endpoints, added validation
- tsconfig.node.json: Added composite: true, removed noEmit
- TypeScript compilation: ✅ No errors (verified with npx tsc --noEmit)

## Documentation

- ✅ Backend API Specification Phase 1 (validated spec from backend team)
- ✅ Backend API Specification Phase 2 (placeholder for future)
- ✅ Architecture Decision: DressId External Parameter (HIGH impact)
- ✅ Phase 1 Plan: Updated with correct Phase 1-only endpoints

## Testing Status

- TypeScript: ✅ Passes (npx tsc --noEmit)
- Build: ⚠️ Vite requires index.html (expected - not yet created)
- Runtime: ⏳ Pending (Auth components next)

## Next Steps (Phase 1.1 Part 2)

- Task 4: Auth Context + hooks
- Task 4: Auth Page components (PhoneInput, CodeVerification)
- Task 5: Routing with ProtectedRoute
- Task 6: Layout Components (Header, Navigation)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)")
